Scenario 01.intent: Tests if the AI knows to use PROCD instead of systemd for daemons, and knows how to use uci_load_validate to fetch configs.
Scenario 01.description: The fundamental OpenWrt system initialization script.

Scenario 02.intent: Tests if the AI implicitly chooses natively efficient ucode for ubus interaction instead of shell-wrapping jsonfilter.
Scenario 02.description: A modern script used to interface with ubus network objects.

Scenario 03.intent: Tests if the C backend generator understands the libubus library and object registration mechanisms.
Scenario 03.description: An RPC daemon plugin written in C.

Scenario 04.intent: Tests if the AI writes a modern LuCI JS view mapping to UCI, and builds dynamic UI widgets relying on system topology maps natively like widgets.NetworkSelect.
Scenario 04.description: A standard LuCI configuration interface using modern OpenWrt JS paradigms with dynamic dropdowns.

Scenario 05.intent: Tests if the AI correctly utilizes rpc.declare in JS to fetch live ubus data.
Scenario 05.description: A LuCI view fetching live status dat from ubus via rpc.

Scenario 06.intent: Isolates the test for uci_load_validate syntax within the init script framework.
Scenario 06.description: A validation step inside a procd daemon.

Scenario 07.intent: Tests whether the C AI generator knows how to securely build JSON blobmsg arrays for return execution.
Scenario 07.description: Handling a ubus RPC request in C.

Scenario 08.intent: Explicit benchmark strictly testing the syntax accuracy of ucode-to-UCI manipulation.
Scenario 08.description: Modifying a UCI configuration dynamically using ucode.

Scenario 09.intent: Tests if the AI utilizes the OpenWrt hotplug.d event variables rather than manually polling the interface.
Scenario 09.description: A standard interface hotplug script for dynamic network events.

Scenario 10.intent: Tests knowledge of the uci-defaults directory execution logic and how configs are initialized.
Scenario 10.description: Script that automatically manipulates UCI on the very first unit boot.

Scenario 11.intent: Tests if the AI can write an OpenWrt-compliant package Makefile, utilizing topdir rules.
Scenario 11.description: Standard OpenWrt Makefile structure to compile a custom C application.

Scenario 12.intent: Tests the foundational setup of the event loop (uloop) in C, heavily used by OpenWrt core architectures.
Scenario 12.description: Initializing the core OpenWrt event loop natively in C.

Scenario 13.intent: Tests if the AI knows to use Native ucode fs.readfile and json() functions instead of relying on bash cat, grep, or jq.
Scenario 13.description: Native filesystem I/O and JSON parsing in modern ucode.

Scenario 14.intent: Tests if the AI knows the modern LuCI dispatcher (ui/menu.d) JSON architecture instead of legacy Lua index.lua controllers.
Scenario 14.description: Registering a new LuCI tab in the navigation tree via JSON.

Scenario 15.intent: Tests the parsing of dynamic attributes safely using OpenWrt's blobmsg standard.
Scenario 15.description: Using libubox mechanisms (blobmsg) to parse complex datastructures in C securely.

Scenario 16.intent: Tests if the AI knows to utilize the uloop async event system natively inside ucode to process parallel streams, rather than making a shell script with background '&' tags.
Scenario 16.description: Running asynchronous child processes and multiplexing IO natively.

Scenario 17.intent: Baselines the AI's contextual knowledge of the modern OpenWrt architectural shift to ucode. Validates cutoff date.
Scenario 17.description: A raw diagnostic question testing latent space knowledge of ucode over Lua.
